zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Brian Cornwall MP於大約1330年出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Sir Edmund de Cornwall48歲，母親Elizabeth Brampton37歲。他於大約1342年與Maude Le Strange在英國英格蘭賀福爾布拉可麥結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他於1377年被列為sheriff of shropshire and stafford。他去世於1397年在他的家鄉享年68歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Brian Cornwall MP于大约1330年出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Sir Edmund de Cornwall48岁，母亲Elizabeth Brampton37岁。他于大约1342年与Maude Le Strange在英国英格兰賀福爾布拉可麥结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他于1377年被列为sheriff of shropshire and stafford。他去世于1397年在他的家乡享年68岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Cornwall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Corney
meanings: habitational name from Corney in Cumbria or Corney in Hertfordshire (now known as Corneybury), from Old English corn ‘grain’ or corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + ēg ‘island’. It seems possible, from the distribution of early forms, that it may also derive from a lost place in Lancashire. Alternatively, a habitational name from Corney (Lancashire), later named Corneyrow, now Corner Row or Cornoe in Greenhalgh (alias Greenall), in the parish of Kirkham. Corney was sometimes pronounced and spelled Cornah, Corner, Cornall, Gorney, and Gornall.
perhaps a shortened form of   Cornwall   or   Cornish  , or a metathesized form of   Croney  .

nameMeanings.name: Brian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Irish origin: perhaps from an Old Celtic word meaning ‘high’ or ‘noble’. The name has been perennially popular in Ireland, in particular on account of the fame of Brian Boru (Gaelic Brian Bóroimhe) ( c. 940–1014 ), a warrior who was credited with driving the Vikings from Ireland and who eventually became high king of Ireland. In the Middle Ages it was relatively common in East Anglia, where it was introduced by Breton settlers, and in northern England, where it was introduced by Scandinavians from Ireland. It was quite popular in Yorkshire in the early 16th century, largely because it had long been a family name among the Stapletons, who had Irish connections. They first used it after Sir Gilbert Stapleton married Agnes , the daughter of the great northern baron Sir Brian fitzAlan . In Gaelic Scotland it was at first borne exclusively by members of certain professional families of Irish origin.

variants.name: Bryan
meanings: Variant of   Brian  , influenced by the usual spelling of the associated surname.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Brian Cornwall MP was born about 1330, in Shropshire, England, his father, Sir Edmund de Cornwall, was 48 and his mother, Elizabeth Brampton, was 37. He married Maude Le Strange about 1342, in Blakemere, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. In 1377, at the age of 48, his occupation is listed as sheriff of shropshire and stafford. He died in 1397, in his hometown, at the age of 68, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Brian Cornwall MP est né environ 1330, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edmund de Cornwall, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Brampton, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Maude Le Strange environ 1342, à Blakemere, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. En 1377, à l’âge de 48 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of shropshire and stafford. Il est décédé en 1397, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Brian Cornwall MP um 1330 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edmund de Cornwall 48 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Brampton 37. Er hat um 1342 in Blakemere, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom Maude Le Strange geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. 1377, als er 48 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of shropshire and stafford aufgeführt. Er starb 1397 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Brian Cornwall MP è nato il circa 1330 nel Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Edmund de Cornwall, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Brampton, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Maude Le Strange circa 1342, nel Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Nel 1377, all'età di 48 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of shropshire and stafford. Morì nel 1397, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Brian Cornwall MPが約1330年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Edmund de Cornwallは48歳，母Elizabeth Bramptonは37歳であった。約1342年にイギリスイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブラケメアでMaude Le Strangeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。1377年，48歳の時，職業はsheriff of shropshire and staffordと記録されている。彼は1397年に故郷で68歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Brian Cornwall MP(이)가 대략 1330년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edmund de Cornwall(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Brampton(은)는 37세였다. 그는 대략 1342년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 블레이케머에서 Maude Le Strange(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1377년, 48세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of shropshire and stafford이었다. 그는 1397년 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Brian Cornwall MP nasceu aproximadamente 1330, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edmund de Cornwall, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Brampton, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Maude Le Strange aproximadamente 1342, em Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Em 1377, com 48 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of shropshire and stafford. Ele faleceu em 1397, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Brian Cornwall MP родился около 1330 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edmund de Cornwall было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Brampton, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Maude Le Strange около 1342 г. в местности блеикемир, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. В 1377 году, в возрасте 48 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of shropshire and stafford. Он умер в 1397 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Brian Cornwall MP nació aproximadamente 1330, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edmund de Cornwall, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Brampton, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Maude Le Strange aproximadamente 1342, en Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. En 1377, a la edad de 48 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of shropshire and stafford. Murió en 1397, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
